title: Barabati Fort
tagline: Barabati Fort — heritage city of Odisha

overview.short: Barabati Fort is a 987 CE fort built by Marakata Keshari of Somavanshi (Keshari) dynasty in Cuttack, Odisha.
overview.description: Barabati Fort is a 987 CE fort built by Marakata Keshari of Somavanshi (Keshari) dynasty in Cuttack, Odisha. The ruins of the fort remain with its moat, gate, and the earthen mound of the nine-storied palace.

topPlaces.name: Siege of Barabati (1745)
topPlaces.description: The Siege of Barabati (1745) was a significant military engagement during the Maratha invasions of Bengal. In February 1745, Raghuji Bhonsle instigated by Mustafa Khan advanced toward Orissa, catching Durlabh Ram, the Na’ib Nazim.

topPlaces.name: Siege of Cuttack (1749)
topPlaces.description: The Siege of Cuttack occurred in May 1749 during the Fifth Maratha invasion of Bengal, when Alivardi Khan (r. 1740–1756), Nawab of Bengal, sought to reconquer Orissa which had been under Maratha possession since its capture in 1745. Leaving Murshidabad in mid-March, he assembled troops at Katwa, disbanded mutinous artillery at Burdwan, and advanced to Midnapore with 5,000–6,000 cavalry, forcing Maratha commander Mir Habib to retreat.

topPlaces.name: Archdiocese of Cuttack-Bhubaneswar
topPlaces.description: The Roman Catholic Archdiocese of Cuttack-Bhubaneswar (Latin: Cuttacken(sis)-Bhubanesvaren(sis)) is an archdiocese located in the cities of Cuttack, Bhubaneswar and covering the entire Kandhamal district in India.

topPlaces.name: Barabati Stadium
topPlaces.description: The Barabati Stadium is an Indian sports stadium used mostly for cricket and association football, and also sometimes for concerts and field hockey, located in Cuttack, Odisha. It is a regular venue for international cricket and is the home ground of Odisha cricket team.

topPlaces.name: Cuttack Chandi Temple
topPlaces.description: The Kataka Chandi Temple is a temple dedicated to the Hindu goddess Chandi in Cuttack, Odisha, India, near the banks of the Mahanadi River.

topPlaces.name: Baba Bhusandeswar Temple
topPlaces.description: Baba Bhusandeswar Temple - Asia's Largest Shiva Linga is located at Bhograi Block, Kumbhirgadi village of Balasore district, Odisha, India.

topPlaces.name: 91.9 Sidharth FM
topPlaces.description: 91.9 Sidharth FM, earlier known as Sarthak FM. Sidharth FM radio stations broadcast from Cuttack. This channel provides 24x7 superhit Odia songs and Hindi songs music.

topPlaces.name: Board of Secondary Education, Odisha
topPlaces.description: The Board of Secondary Education, Odisha (abbreviated B.S.E) is a board of education for government, public and private schools under the state government of Odisha, India.

items.note: The Siege of Barabati (1745) was a significant military engagement during the Maratha invasions of Bengal. In February 1745, Raghuji Bhonsle instigated by Mustafa Khan advanced toward Orissa, catching Durlabh Ram, the Na’ib Nazim.

The Siege of Cuttack occurred in May 1749 during the Fifth Maratha invasion of Bengal, when Alivardi Khan (r. 1740–1756), Nawab of Bengal, sought to reconquer Orissa which had been under Maratha possession since its capture in 1745. Leaving Murshidabad in mid-March, he assembled troops at Katwa, disbanded mutinous artillery at Burdwan, and advanced to Midnapore with 5,000–6,000 cavalry, forcing Maratha commander Mir Habib to retreat.

The Roman Catholic Archdiocese of Cuttack-Bhubaneswar (Latin: Cuttacken(sis)-Bhubanesvaren(sis)) is an archdiocese located in the cities of Cuttack, Bhubaneswar and covering the entire Kandhamal district in India.

The Barabati Stadium is an Indian sports stadium used mostly for cricket and association football, and also sometimes for concerts and field hockey, located in Cuttack, Odisha. It is a regular venue for international cricket and is the home ground of Odisha cricket team.
The Kataka Chandi Temple is a temple dedicated to the Hindu goddess Chandi in Cuttack, Odisha, India, near the banks of the Mahanadi River.
Baba Bhusandeswar Temple - Asia's Largest Shiva Linga is located at Bhograi Block, Kumbhirgadi village of Balasore district, Odisha, India.
91.9 Sidharth FM, earlier known as Sarthak FM. Sidharth FM radio stations broadcast from Cuttack. This channel provides 24x7 superhit Odia songs and Hindi songs music.
The Board of Secondary Education, Odisha (abbreviated B.S.E) is a board of education for government, public and private schools under the state government of Odisha, India.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Barabati Fort?
faq.a: The best time to visit Barabati Fort is Oct – Mar. Summers are 26–40°C and winters are 12–28°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Barabati Fort?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Biju Patnaik International Airport, Bhubaneswar (27 km) and the nearest railway station is Bhubaneswar Railway Station (25 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Barabati Fort?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Barabati Fort at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Barabati Fort?
faq.a: Stays in Barabati Fort start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Barabati Fort?
faq.a: The top attractions are Siege of Barabati (1745), Siege of Cuttack (1749), Archdiocese of Cuttack-Bhubaneswar, Barabati Stadium, Cuttack Chandi Temple.
